generatedSentence: A wannabe hero, tired from his day job as a hairdresser and his evening pilates class, found himself gawking at a vintage cinema marquee. He dreamed of a life of importance, far from the blank layers of his current existence. A raffle ticket, almost an official warrant for escape, lay on his knuckle. He imagined a sprawling villa, not the hotels he usually visited, filled with mellifluous music and a gracious host. He felt amped for something new, even if it meant a temporary layover from his mundane disco nights, watching a boring documentary. The hawthorn outside his window seemed to wave, almost urging him on. He was a duckling, internally limpid, yet imagining ultimate adventure. But the path to glory was hard and heavy. A sudden break in the city's powerplant initiated a doomsday scenario, plunging the cyberpunk metropolis into chaos. The tramways stopped, causing a lagging gridlock. A churlish retailer screamed mayday as jackals of the underworld, the nightwalkers, began their hegemony. The situation felt utterly futile, an untenable artificial intelligence threatening a full karyokinesis of society, fueled by a terrifying necromancer. He felt a deep fatigue, seeing how easily this could turn wasteful, like a bad karaoke session. He knew he had to recheck his commitment, despite the menacing wolf in the dark. He shed his noob mentality, realizing the gravity of the inferno. His tenacity was invoked as he watched a majestic violet light flicker from the facility, a sign of the battery’s core being highjacked. Frantically, he gesticulated, directing trackers to the offroad vehicle, a whacky contraption with knobbly tires and antlers. It was a tempting, tantalising moment to give up, but he stood prone, ready to knock on the door of destiny. He knew an omission of action now would ensure a wasteful future. This was the ultimate challenge, no longer a whimsical notion, but a fight for survival against unkind forces, a truly raunchy encounter with pure, unadulterated evil. Through sheer willpower and a cleverly devised workaround, he managed to sever the connections to the system's main valve, causing the display to flicker and die. The doomsday machine, a wrecking ball of geometry and coal-powered witchcraft, began to break down inexorably. Waking from the nightmare, he saw the graveyard of the city beginning to organically rebuild itself. The hardwood floors of the control room were strewn with grapes and lactose-free protein bars he'd devoured. His neighborly spirit, once hidden beneath layers of self-doubt, shone through. He realized his valiant fight had restored balance, proving that even a single stick of resistance could ultimately bring light back to the dark, a true documentary of courage. He wasn't a nutjob, but a hero.

He felt amped for something new, even if it meant a temporary layover from his mundane disco nights, watching a boring documentary. The hawthorn outside his window seemed to wave, almost urging him on. He was a duckling, internally limpid, yet imagining ultimate adventure.

But the path to glory was hard and heavy. A sudden break in the city's powerplant initiated a doomsday scenario, plunging the cyberpunk metropolis into chaos. The tramways stopped, causing a lagging gridlock. A churlish retailer screamed mayday as jackals of the underworld, the nightwalkers, began their hegemony. The situation felt utterly futile, an untenable artificial intelligence threatening a full karyokinesis of society, fueled by a terrifying necromancer. He felt a deep fatigue, seeing how easily this could turn wasteful, like a bad karaoke session. He knew he had to recheck his commitment, despite the menacing wolf in the dark.

He shed his noob mentality, realizing the gravity of the inferno. His tenacity was invoked as he watched a majestic violet light flicker from the facility, a sign of the battery’s core being highjacked. Frantically, he gesticulated, directing trackers to the offroad vehicle, a whacky contraption with knobbly tires and antlers. It was a tempting, tantalising moment to give up, but he stood prone, ready to knock on the door of destiny. He knew an omission of action now would ensure a wasteful future. This was the ultimate challenge, no longer a whimsical notion, but a fight for survival against unkind forces, a truly raunchy encounter with pure, unadulterated evil.

Waking from the nightmare, he saw the graveyard of the city beginning to organically rebuild itself. He realized his valiant fight had restored balance, proving that even a single stick of resistance could ultimately bring light back to the dark, a true documentary of courage. He wasn't a nutjob, but a hero.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a lean figure with short, spiky black hair, wearing a sleek, dark grey tech-suit with glowing blue accents and a single crimson gauntlet. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist wistfully gazes at a holographic cinema marquee, a raffle ticket clutched in his hand, a futuristic cityscape sprawling behind him. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist stands silhouetted against a chaotic, darkened cityscape, tramways halted, ominous red lightning striking a distant powerplant. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist, determined, drives a custom offroad vehicle with visible antlers and knobbly tires, speeding towards a glowing violet facility, his crimson gauntlet raised. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist, victorious but exhausted, stands amidst the wreckage of a massive, geometric machine, sunlight beginning to break through the clouds, holding a single glowing stick.

definitions.definition: Authorization or certification; a sanction, as given by a superior.
Something that provides assurance or confirmation; a guarantee or proof.
definitions.example: a warrant of authenticity; a warrant for success

definitions.definition: An order that serves as authorization; especially a voucher authorizing payment or receipt of money.
An option, usually issued together with another security and with a term at issue greater than a year, to buy other securities of the issuer.
A judicial writ authorizing an officer to make a search, seizure, or arrest, or to execute a judgment.
definitions.example: an arrest warrant issued by the court

definitions.definition: Short for warrant officer.
A document certifying that a motor vehicle meets certain standards of mechanical soundness and safety; a warrant of fitness.
A defender, a protector.
Underclay in a coal mine.
To protect, keep safe (from danger).
To give (someone) an assurance or guarantee (of something); also, with a double object: to guarantee (someone something).
To guarantee (something) to be (of a specified quality, value, etc.).
To guarantee as being true; (colloquially) to believe strongly.
definitions.example: That tree is going to fall, I’ll warrant.

definitions.definition: To authorize; to give (someone) sanction or warrant (to do something).
definitions.example: I am warranted to search these premises fully.

definitions.definition: To justify; to give grounds for.
definitions.example: Circumstances arose that warranted the use of lethal force.

definitions.definition: Someone who wishes to be or do something, but lacks the qualifications or talent; an overeager amateur; an aspirant.
definitions.example: Hollywood's restaurants are full of wannabe actors waiting to be discovered.

definitions.definition: Someone who wishes to be part of, or to assimilate to, a majority group of which they are not a member.
An instance of breaking something into two or more pieces.
definitions.example: The femur has a clean break and so should heal easily.

definitions.definition: A physical space that opens up in something or between two things.
definitions.example: He waited minutes for a break in the traffic to cross the highway.

definitions.definition: A rest or pause, usually from work.
definitions.example: Let’s take a five-minute break.

definitions.definition: A time for students to talk or play.
A short holiday.
definitions.example: a weekend break on the Isle of Wight

definitions.definition: A temporary split with a romantic partner.
definitions.example: I think we need a break.

definitions.definition: An interval or intermission between two parts of a performance, for example a theatre show, broadcast, or sports game.
A significant change in circumstance, attitude, perception, or focus of attention.
definitions.example: big break

definitions.definition: The beginning (of the morning).
definitions.example: at the break of day

definitions.definition: An act of escaping.
definitions.example: It was a clean break.

definitions.definition: The separation between lines, paragraphs or pages of a written text.
A keystroke or other signal that causes a program to terminate or suspend execution.
A point or condition in a program at which operation may be suspended during debugging so that the state of the program at that point can be investigated. A breakpoint.
A change, particularly the end of a spell of persistent good or bad weather.
A large four-wheeled carriage, having a straight body and calash top, with the driver's seat in front and the footman's behind.
(equitation) A sharp bit or snaffle.
A short section of music, often between verses, in which some performers stop while others continue.
definitions.example: The fiddle break was amazing; it was a pity the singer came back in on the wrong note.

definitions.definition: The point in the musical scale at which a woodwind instrument is designed to overblow, that is, to move from its lower to its upper register.
definitions.example: Crossing the break smoothly is one of the first lessons the young clarinettist needs to master.

definitions.definition: Usu. plural An area along a river that features steep banks, bluffs, or gorges (e.g., Upper Missouri River Breaks National Monument, US).
To separate into two or more pieces, to fracture or crack, by a process that cannot easily be reversed for reassembly.
definitions.example: If the vase falls to the floor, it might break.

definitions.definition: To divide (something, often money) into smaller units.
definitions.example: Can you break a hundred-dollar bill for me?

definitions.definition: To cause (a person or animal) to lose spirit or will; to crush the spirits of.
definitions.example: Her child's death broke Angela.

definitions.definition: To be crushed, or overwhelmed with sorrow or grief.
definitions.example: My heart is breaking.

definitions.definition: To interrupt; to destroy the continuity of; to dissolve or terminate.
definitions.example: I had won four games in a row, but now you've broken my streak of luck.

definitions.definition: To ruin financially.
definitions.example: The recession broke some small businesses.

definitions.definition: To violate, to not adhere to.
definitions.example: He broke his vows by cheating on his wife.

definitions.definition: (of a fever) To pass the most dangerous part of the illness; to go down, in terms of temperature.
definitions.example: Susan's fever broke at about 3 AM, and the doctor said the worst was over.

definitions.definition: (of a spell of settled weather) To end.
definitions.example: The forecast says the hot weather will break by midweek.

definitions.definition: (of a storm) To begin; to end.
definitions.example: Around midday the storm broke, and the afternoon was calm and sunny.

definitions.definition: (of morning, dawn, day etc.) To arrive.
definitions.example: Morning has broken.

definitions.definition: To render (a game) unchallenging by altering its rules or exploiting loopholes or weaknesses in them in a way that gives a player an unfair advantage.
definitions.example: Changing the rules to let white have three extra queens would break chess.

definitions.definition: To stop, or to cause to stop, functioning properly or altogether.
definitions.example: Did you two break the trolley by racing with it?

definitions.definition: To cause (a barrier) to no longer bar.
definitions.example: break a seal

definitions.definition: To destroy the arrangement of; to throw into disorder; to pierce.
definitions.example: The cavalry were not able to break the British squares.

definitions.definition: (of a wave of water) To collapse into surf, after arriving in shallow water.
To burst forth; to make its way; to come into view.
To interrupt or cease one's work or occupation temporarily.
definitions.example: Let's break for lunch.

definitions.definition: To interrupt (a fall) by inserting something so that the falling object does not (immediately) hit something else beneath.
definitions.example: He survived the jump out the window because the bushes below broke his fall.

definitions.definition: To disclose or make known an item of news, etc.
definitions.example: I don't know how to break this to you, but your cat is not coming back.

definitions.definition: (of a sound) To become audible suddenly.
To change a steady state abruptly.
definitions.example: His coughing broke the silence.

definitions.definition: To suddenly become.
definitions.example: The arrest was standard, when suddenly the suspect broke ugly.

definitions.definition: Of a male voice, to become deeper at puberty.
Of a voice, to alter in type due to emotion or strain: in men generally to go up, in women sometimes to go down; to crack.
definitions.example: His voice breaks when he gets emotional.

definitions.definition: To surpass or do better than (a specific number), to do better than (a record), setting a new record.
definitions.example: He broke the men's 100-meter record.

definitions.definition: (most often in the passive tense) To demote, to reduce the military rank of.
To end (a connection), to disconnect.
definitions.example: I couldn't hear a thing he was saying, so I broke the connection and called him back.

definitions.definition: (of an emulsion) To demulsify.
To counter-attack
To lay open, as a purpose; to disclose, divulge, or communicate.
To become weakened in constitution or faculties; to lose health or strength.
To fail in business; to become bankrupt.
To destroy the strength, firmness, or consistency of.
definitions.example: to break flax

definitions.definition: To destroy the official character and standing of; to cashier; to dismiss.
To make an abrupt or sudden change; to change the gait.
definitions.example: to break into a run or gallop

definitions.definition: To fall out; to terminate friendship.
To terminate the execution of a program before normal completion.
To suspend the execution of a program during debugging so that the state of the program can be investigated.
A small French coin, originally of silver, afterwards of copper, worth 5 deniers; also a silver coin of Henry V current in the parts of France then held by the English, worth about 8 pence .
A nonplus .
The white spot in the centre of a target; hence the object to which anything is directed or aimed, the range of such aim .
A lot by which nothing is gained; a ticket in a lottery on which no prize is indicated .
An empty space; a void, for example on a paper .

(now chiefly U.S.) A document, paper, or form with spaces left blank to be filled up at the pleasure of the person to whom it is given (e.g. a blank charter, ballot, form, contract, etc.), or as the event may determine; a blank form .
Blank verse .
A piece of metal (such as a coin, screw, nuts), cut and shaped to the required size of the thing to be made, and ready for the finishing operations; (coining) the disc of metal before stamping .
A vacant space, place, or period; a void .
The 1 / 230400 of a grain .
An empty space in one's memory; a forgotten item or memory .
A dash written in place of an omitted letter or word
The space character; the character resulting from pressing the space-bar on a keyboard.
A domino without points on one or both of its divisions.
definitions.example: the double blank

definitions.definition: Short for blank cartridge. .
(in the expression ‘shooting blanks’) An ineffective effort which achieves nothing .
To make void; to erase.
definitions.example: I blanked out my previous entry.

definitions.definition: To ignore (a person) deliberately.
definitions.example: She blanked me for no reason.

definitions.definition: To prevent from scoring, for example in a sporting event.
definitions.example: England blanks Wales to advance to the final.

definitions.definition: To become blank.
To be temporarily unable to remember.
definitions.example: I'm blanking on her name right now.

definitions.definition: White or pale; without colour.
Free from writing, printing, or marks; having an empty space to be filled in
definitions.example: a blank ballot

definitions.definition: Scoreless; without any goals or points.
Lacking characteristics which give variety; uniform.
definitions.example: a blank desert; a blank wall; blank unconsciousness

definitions.definition: Absolute; downright; sheer.
definitions.example: There was a look of blank terror on his face.

definitions.definition: Without expression.
definitions.example: Failing to understand the question, he gave me a blank stare.

definitions.definition: Utterly confounded or discomfited.
Empty; void; without result; fruitless.
definitions.example: a blank day

definitions.definition: Devoid of thoughts, memory, or inspiration.
definitions.example: The shock left his memory blank.

definitions.definition: Of ammunition: having propellant but no bullets; unbulleted.
definitions.example: The recruits were issued with blank rounds for a training exercise.

definitions.definition: A single thickness of some material covering a surface.
definitions.example: After the first coat of paint dried, he applied another layer.

definitions.definition: A (usually) horizontal deposit; a stratum.
definitions.example: I find seven-layer cake a bit too rich.

definitions.definition: One of the items in a hierarchy.
definitions.example: mired in layers of deceit

definitions.definition: (by analogy to a stack of transparencies) one in a stack of (initially transparent) drawing surfaces that comprise an image; used to keep elements of an image separate so that they can be modified independently from one another.
To cut or divide (something) into layers
To arrange (something) in layers.
definitions.example: Layer the ribbons on top of one another to make an attractive pattern.

definitions.definition: A person who lays things, such as tiles.
A mature female bird, insect, etc. that is able to lay eggs.
definitions.example: When dealing with an infestation of headlice, the first step is to eliminate the layers.

definitions.definition: A hen kept to lay eggs.
A shoot of a plant, laid underground for growth.
A physical fitness system developed in the early 20th century by Joseph Pilates.
To call upon (a person, a god) for help, assistance or guidance.
To solicit, petition for, appeal to a favorable attitude.
definitions.example: The envoy invoked the King of Kings's magnanimity to reduce his province's tribute after another drought.

definitions.definition: To call to mind (something) for some purpose.
To appeal for validation to a (notably cited) authority.
definitions.example: In certain Christian circles, invoking the Bible constitutes irrefutable proof.

definitions.definition: To conjure up with incantations.
definitions.example: This satanist ritual invokes Beelzebub.

definitions.definition: To bring about as an inevitable consequence.
definitions.example: Blasphemy is taboo as it may invoke divine wrath.

definitions.definition: To cause (a program or subroutine) to execute.
definitions.example: Interactive programs let the users enter choices and invoke the corresponding routines.

definitions.definition: Relating to, or having the characteristics of, an icon.
Famously and distinctively representative of its type.
Representing something; symbolic.
definitions.example: an iconic gesture in sign language

definitions.definition: A person who cuts or styles hair as an occupation or profession.
A villain or bad guy; the one responsible for evil or aggressive acts.
definitions.example: With his wrinkled, uneven face, the actor always seemed to play the heavy in films.

definitions.definition: A doorman, bouncer or bodyguard.
definitions.example: A fight started outside the bar but the heavies came out and stopped it.

definitions.definition: A large multi-engined aircraft. (The term heavy normally follows the call-sign when used by air traffic controllers.)
(often with "up") To make heavier.
To sadden.
To use power and/or wealth to exert influence on, e.g., governments or corporations; to pressure.
definitions.example: The union was well known for the methods it used to heavy many businesses.

definitions.definition: (of a physical object) Having great weight.
(of a topic) Serious, somber.
Not easy to bear; burdensome; oppressive.
definitions.example: heavy yokes, expenses, undertakings, trials, news, etc.

definitions.definition: Good.
definitions.example: This film is heavy.

definitions.definition: (late 1960s, 1970s) Profound.
definitions.example: The Moody Blues are, like, heavy.

definitions.definition: (of a rate of flow) High, great.
Armed.
definitions.example: Come heavy, or not at all.

definitions.definition: Louder, more distorted.
definitions.example: Metal is heavier than swing.

definitions.definition: (of weather) Hot and humid.
(of a person) Doing the specified activity more intensely than most other people.
definitions.example: He was a heavy sleeper, a heavy eater and a heavy smoker – certainly not an ideal husband.

definitions.definition: (of food) High in fat or protein; difficult to digest.
definitions.example: Cheese-stuffed sausage is too heavy to eat before exercising.

definitions.definition: Of great force, power, or intensity; deep or intense.
definitions.example: it was a heavy storm;  a heavy slumber in bed;  a heavy punch

definitions.definition: Laden to a great extent.
definitions.example: his eyes were heavy with sleep;  she was heavy with child

definitions.definition: Laden with that which is weighty; encumbered; burdened; bowed down, either with an actual burden, or with grief, pain, disappointment, etc.
Slow; sluggish; inactive; or lifeless, dull, inanimate, stupid.
definitions.example: a heavy gait, looks, manners, style, etc.

definitions.definition: Impeding motion; cloggy; clayey.
definitions.example: a heavy road; a heavy soil

definitions.definition: Not raised or leavened.
definitions.example: heavy bread

definitions.definition: (of wines or spirits) Having much body or strength.
With child; pregnant.
Containing one or more isotopes that are heavier than the normal one
(petroleum) with high viscosity
In a heavy manner; weightily; heavily; gravely.
definitions.example: heavy laden with their sins

definitions.definition: To a great degree; greatly.
Very
A firm or paved beach or slope convenient for hauling vessels out of the water.
A tyre whose compound is softer than superhards, and harder than mediums.
Crack cocaine.
Hard labor.
definitions.example: The prisoners were sentenced to three years' hard.

definitions.definition: (of material or fluid) Having a severe property; presenting difficulty.
(personal or social) Having a severe property; presenting difficulty.
Unquestionable.
definitions.example: hard evidence;  a hard requirement

definitions.definition: (of a road intersection) Having a comparatively larger or a ninety-degree angle.
definitions.example: At the intersection, there are two roads going to the left. Take the hard left.

definitions.definition: (of a male) Sexually aroused.
definitions.example: I got so hard watching two hot guys wrestle each other on the beach.

definitions.definition: Having muscles that are tightened as a result of intense, regular exercise.
Plosive.
definitions.example: There is a hard c in "clock" and a soft c in "centre".

definitions.definition: Unvoiced
definitions.example: Hard k, t, s, ch, as distinguished from soft, g, d, z, j.

definitions.definition: Velarized or plain, rather than palatalized
definitions.example: The letter ж in Russian is always hard.

definitions.definition: Having a severe property; presenting a barrier to enjoyment.
In a physical form, not digital.
definitions.example: a soft or hard copy ; a digital or hard archive

definitions.definition: Using a manual or physical process, not by means of a software command.
definitions.example: a hard reboot or reset

definitions.definition: Far, extreme.
definitions.example: hard right, hard left

definitions.definition: Of silk: not having had the natural gum boiled off.
(manner) With much force or effort.
definitions.example: At the intersection, bear hard left.

definitions.definition: (manner) With difficulty.
definitions.example: His degree was hard earned.

definitions.definition: So as to raise difficulties.
(manner) Compactly.
definitions.example: The lake had finally frozen hard.

definitions.definition: Near, close.
Incapable of producing results; doomed not to be successful; not worth attempting.
Flowing like honey.
Sweet, smooth and musical; pleasant to hear (generally used of a person's voice, tone or writing style).
A movie theatre, a movie house
definitions.example: The cinema is right across the street from the restaurant.

definitions.definition: Films collectively.
definitions.example: Despite the critics, he produced excellent cinema.

definitions.definition: The film and movie industry.
definitions.example: In the long history of Spanish cinema [...] .

definitions.definition: The art of making films and movies; cinematography
definitions.example: Throughout the history of cinema, filmmakers [...] .

definitions.definition: Any of the joints between the phalanges of the fingers.
(by extension) A mechanical joint.
A cut of meat.
The curved part of the cushion at the entrance to the pockets on a cue sports table.
The kneejoint of a quadruped, especially of a calf; formerly used of the kneejoint of a human being.
The joint of a plant.
A convex portion of a vessel's figure where a sudden change of shape occurs, as in a canal boat, where a nearly vertical side joins a nearly flat bottom.
A contrivance, usually of brass or iron, and furnished with points, worn to protect the hand, to add force to a blow, and to disfigure the person struck; a knuckle duster.
definitions.example: brass knuckles

definitions.definition: The rounded point where a flat changes to a slope on a piste.
To apply pressure, or rub or massage with one's knuckles.
definitions.example: He knuckled the sleep from his eyes.

definitions.definition: To bend the fingers.
To touch one's forehead as a mark of respect.
To yield.
A drawing, often held as a fundraiser, in which tickets or chances are sold to win a prize.
definitions.example: He entered a raffle to win a lifetime supply of toothpaste, but he did not win.

definitions.definition: A game of dice in which the player who throws three of the same number wins all the stakes.
To award something by means of a raffle or random drawing, often used with off.
definitions.example: They raffled off four gift baskets.

definitions.definition: To participate in a raffle.
definitions.example: to raffle for a watch

definitions.definition: The most basic or fundamental of a set of things
The final or most distant point; the conclusion
The greatest extremity; the maximum
A non-contact competitive team sport played with a 175 gram flying disc, the object of which is to score points by passing the disc to a player in the opposing end zone; ultimate frisbee.
To finish; to complete.
Final; last in a series.
(of a syllable) Last in a word or other utterance.
Being the greatest possible; maximum; most extreme.
definitions.example: the ultimate disappointment

definitions.definition: Being the most distant or extreme; farthest.
That will happen at some time; eventual.
Last in a train of progression or consequences; tended toward by all that precedes; arrived at, as the last result; final.
Incapable of further analysis; incapable of further division or separation; constituent; elemental.
definitions.example: an ultimate constituent of matter

definitions.definition: (usually with up) To amplify.
definitions.example: He asked the disk jockey to amp it up.

definitions.definition: (usually with up) To excite.
(usually with up) To intensify or increase.
definitions.example: 2014, Andy Paul, Amp Up Your Sales, AMACOM Books

definitions.definition: A device that controls the flow of a gas or fluid through a pipe.
A device that admits fuel and air into the cylinder of an internal combustion engine, or one that allows combustion gases to exit.
One or more membranous partitions, flaps, or folds, which permit the passage of the contents of a vessel or cavity in one direction, but stop or control the flow in the opposite direction
definitions.example: the ileocolic, mitral, and semilunar valves

definitions.definition: One of the leaves of a folding-door, or a window-sash.
A vacuum tube.
One of the pieces into which certain fruits naturally separate when they dehisce.
A small portion of certain anthers, which opens like a trapdoor to allow the pollen to escape, such as in the barberry.
One of the pieces or divisions of bivalve or multivalve shells.
One of the two similar portions of the shell of a diatom.
To control (flow) by means of a valve.
(plural "villas") A house, often larger and more expensive than average, in the countryside or on the coast, often used as a retreat.
(plural "villas") A family house, often semi-detached, in a middle class street.
(plural "villae") A country house, with farm buildings around a courtyard.
To destroy violently; to cause severe damage to something, to a point where it no longer works, or is useless.
definitions.example: He wrecked the car in a collision.

definitions.definition: To ruin or dilapidate.
To dismantle wrecked vehicles or other objects, to reclaim any useful parts.
To involve in a wreck; hence, to cause to suffer ruin; to balk of success, and bring disaster on.
The act by which something is wrecked.
The taking of valuables from a shipwreck close to the shore.
Given to whimsy.
To fail to keep up (the pace), to fall behind
To cover (for example, pipes) with felt strips or similar material (referring to a time lag effect in thermal transfer)
To transport as a punishment for crime.
To cause to lag; to slacken.
The covering of something with strips of felt, wood etc, either as insulation or for protection.
The material so used.
A prison sentence.
Falling behind, not keeping up the pace
Occurring after; indicating the later phase of
A station built for the production of electric power; a power station.
The portion of a vehicle devoted to providing energy for motion; the engine or motor.
A young duck.
The day when God is expected to judge the world; end times.
Judgement day; the day of the Final Judgment; any day of decisive judgement or final dissolution.
Concerned with or predicting future universal destruction.
Given to or marked by forebodings or predictions of impending calamity.
Capable of causing widespread or total destruction.
To stare or gape stupidly.
To stare conspicuously.
Kind and warmly courteous
Tactful
Compassionate
Indulgent, charming and graceful
Elegant and with good taste
Benignant
Full of grace
Expression of surprise, contempt, outrage, disgust, boredom, frustration.
Any of certain wild canids of the genus Canis, native to the tropical Old World and smaller than a wolf.
A person who performs menial/routine tasks, a dogsbody.
A person who behaves in an opportunistic way; especially a base collaborator.
A jack (the playing card).
To perform menial or routine tasks
To apply ink to; to cover or smear with ink.
To sign (a contract or similar document).
To apply a tattoo to (someone).
(of a squid or octopus) to eject ink (sense 3)
An application of ink.
Work done by an inker, one kind of commercial artist
Inputting text or drawings into a computer, tablet, or similar apparatus using a stylus.
A defensive activity where ink is released by squids, cuttlefish, etc.
The quality or condition of being important or worthy of note.
Significance or prominence.
Personal status or standing.
Something of importance.
A place or situation resembling Hell.
A large fire, a conflagration.
A large town house or mansion; a grand private residence, especially in France.
An establishment that provides accommodation and other services for paying guests; normally larger than a guesthouse, and often one of a chain.
A restaurant; any dining establishment.
The letter H in the ICAO spelling alphabet.
The larger red property in the game of Monopoly, in contradistinction to houses.
The guest accommodation and dining section of a cruise ship.
Domination, influence, or authority over another, especially by one political group over a society or by one nation over others.
Dominance of one social group over another, such that the ruling group or hegemon acquires some degree of consent from the subordinate, as opposed to dominance purely by force.
A weariness caused by exertion; exhaustion.
(often in the plural) A menial task or tasks, especially in the military.
Material failure, such as cracking or separation, caused by stress on the material.
To tire or make weary by physical or mental exertion
To wilt a salad by dressing or tossing it
To lose so much strength or energy that one becomes tired, weary, feeble or exhausted
(of a material specimen) to undergo the process of fatigue; to fail as a result of fatigue.
An international distress signal used by shipping and aircraft
The track on which a tram (streetcar) runs.
The system of cables that supports a cable car.
The process of change that takes place during the division of a cell nucleus at mitosis or meiosis.

A form of entertainment popular in clubs, at parties, etc, in which individual members of the public sing along to pre-recorded instrumental versions of popular songs, the lyrics of which are displayed for the singer on a screen in time with the music.
A karaoke session.
To perform karaoke
Smutty; indecent.
Lecherous.
Sexually seductive.
Very low-class; inferior; inadequate.
With dishonorable, base and vulgar expression.
The act of checking again; reverification.

definitions.definition: To check again.
Indicating the last item.

definitions.definition: Indicating the most important action.
synonyms: at the end of the day
when all is said and done
definitions.example: Ultimately, he will have to make a decision before the end of the week.

definitions.definition: Used to indicate the etymon at which a given etymological derivation terminates.
definitions.example: Cognate to Gottscheerish boßər, bàsser. Ultimately cognate to standard High German Wasser.

synonyms: at the end of the day
when all is said and done

definitions.definition: Completely, entirely, to the fullest extent
definitions.example: I have failed you utterly.

definitions.definition: A person who acts with valor, showing hero-like characteristics in the midst of danger.
Showing courage or determination; brave, heroic.
Inclined to waste or squander money or resources.
Uninhabited, desolate.
A spin in which the head and shoulders are dropped backwards and the back arched downwards toward the ice. Also called a layback spin
A maneuver to scale a vertical crack, by pulling with the arms and jamming the legs underneath, and shuffling up on limb at a time.
A surf maneuver where the upright surfer leans back against the wave.
A trick where the boarder leans back while on the lip of the ramp, or down a rail.
(audio editing) The process of recording the final sound onto the master track.
A short backwards pass to a teammate in an attacking position
A wedge-shaped kerbstone that allows a vehicle to enter a driveway without a bump
To climb using layback maneuvers.
A genre of dance music that was popular in the 1970s, characterized by elements of soul music with a strong Latin-American beat and often accompanied by pulsating lights.
To dance disco-style dances.
To go to discotheques.
(slightly obsolete) A nightclub where dancing takes place.
A film, TV program, publication etc. which presents a social, political, scientific or historical subject in a factual or informative manner.
Of, related to, or based on documents.
Which serves to document (record and:or illustrate) a subject.
(of a film, book etc) Presented objectively without the insertion of fictional matter.
A woman with an excessive libido.

definitions.definition: (of a woman) Having an excessive libido.
A person who practices or performs necromancy.
Someone who is crazy (insane).

definitions.definition: (often pejorative) A newb or newbie; refers to the idea that someone is new to a game, concept, or idea; implying a lack of experience. Also, in some areas the word noob can mean someone is obsessed with things.
definitions.example: Noobs are annoying, they never know the forum rules.

definitions.definition: To form a mental image of something; to envision or create something in one's mind.
definitions.example: Try to imagine a pink elephant.

definitions.definition: To believe in something created by one's own mind.
definitions.example: She imagined that the man wanted to kill her.

definitions.definition: To assume
definitions.example: I imagine that he will need to rest after such a long flight.

definitions.definition: To conjecture or guess
definitions.example: I cannot even imagine what you are up to!

definitions.definition: To use one's imagination
definitions.example: Imagine that we were siblings.

definitions.definition: To contrive in purpose; to scheme; to devise.
Something imagined; a figment of the imagination.
In an inexorable manner; without the possibility of stopping or prevention.
definitions.example: We watched as the storm clouds advanced inexorably closer to us.

definitions.definition: Any of various shrubs and small trees of the genus Crataegus having small, apple-like fruits and thorny branches
The quality or state of being tenacious, or persistence of purpose; tenaciousness.
The quality of bodies which keeps them from parting without considerable force, as distinguished from brittleness, fragility, mobility, etc.
The effect of this attraction, cohesiveness.
The quality of bodies which makes them adhere to other bodies; adhesiveness, viscosity.

The greatest longitudinal stress a substance can bear without tearing asunder, usually expressed with reference to a unit area of the cross section of the substance, as the number of pounds per square inch, or kilograms per square centimeter, necessary to produce rupture.
Agent noun of track; one who, or that which, tracks or pursues, as a man or dog that follows game.
In an organ, a light strip of wood connecting (in path) a key and a pallet, to communicate motion by pulling.
A type of computer software for composing music by aligning samples on parallel timelines.

A computer program that monitors something.
A tracker mortgage.
The act of omitting.
The act of neglecting to perform an action one has an obligation to do.
Something deleted or left out.

definitions.definition: Something not done or neglected.
(grammar) The shortening of a word or phrase, using an apostrophe ( ' ) to replace the missing letters, often used to approximate the sound of speech or a specific dialect.
In an organic manner.
In the manner of a living organism.
As part of an organized whole.
Inherently, naturally or constitutionally.
From, or using organic matter.
With regard to organic husbandry.
Having a covering of small knobs.
Sudden fatigue as a result of glycogen depletion from not having taken in enough nutrition.
An abrupt rapping sound, as from an impact of a hard object against wood.

definitions.definition: A sharp impact.

definitions.definition: Criticism.
Preignition, a type of abnormal combustion occurring in spark ignition engines caused by self-ignition; also, the characteristic knocking sound associated with it.
A batsman's innings.

definitions.definition: To strike for admittance; to rap upon, as a door.
To criticize verbally; to denigrate; to undervalue.

definitions.definition: To kick a ball towards another player; to pass.
To impress forcibly or strongly; to astonish; to move to admiration or applause.
To bump or impact.

definitions.definition: To rap one's knuckles against something, especially wood.

definitions.definition: Not able to be held, as of an opinion or position; unholdable, indefensible.

definitions.definition: Unfit for habitation
Man-made; of artifice.
definitions.example: The flowers were artificial, and he thought them rather tacky.

definitions.definition: False, misleading.

definitions.definition: Unnatural.
The gray wolf, specifically all subspecies of the gray wolf (Canis lupus) that are not dingoes or dogs.

definitions.definition: A man who makes amorous advances to many women.
A wolf tone or wolf note.

A white worm, or maggot, which infests granaries.
An eating ulcer or sore. See lupus.
A willying machine.
To devour; to gobble; to eat (something) voraciously.
To make amorous advances to many women; to hit on women; to cruise for sex.
To hunt for wolves.
To relinquish (a right etc.); to give up claim to; to forego.

definitions.definition: To put aside, avoid.
To outlaw (someone).
To abandon, give up (someone or something).
To move from side to side; to sway.
To stray, wander.
To move back and forth repeatedly and somewhat loosely.

definitions.definition: To move one’s hand back and forth (generally above the shoulders) in greeting or departure.
(metonymic) To call attention to, or give a direction or command to, by a waving motion, as of the hand; to signify by waving; to beckon; to signal; to indicate.
definitions.example: I waved goodbye from across the room.

definitions.definition: To have an undulating or wavy form.
To raise into inequalities of surface; to give an undulating form or surface to.
To produce waves to the hair.

definitions.definition: To cause to move back and forth repeatedly.

To move like a wave, or by floating; to waft.
The motion of something that waves.
Repeated moving of arms or hands to signal.

A break between stages of a journey.
definitions.example: We had a layover while waiting to change planes, so we stretched our legs.

definitions.definition: An elongated piece of wood or similar material, typically put to some use, for example as a wand or baton.
Any roughly cylindrical (or rectangular) unit of a substance.

definitions.definition: Material or objects attached to a stick or the like.
A tool, control, or instrument shaped somewhat like a stick.
A stick-like item:
Ability; specifically:
A person or group of people. (Perhaps, in some senses, because people are, broadly speaking, tall and thin, like pieces of wood.)
Encouragement or punishment, or (resulting) vigour or other improved behavior.
A measure.

A show or spectacle.

definitions.definition: A tract of land in which the dead are buried.

(mostly in botany and forestry) The wood from any dicotyledonous tree, without regard to its hardness.

definitions.definition: The tree or tree species that yields the preceding.
definitions.example: This hardwood has been planted extensively throughout the hills here.

definitions.definition: A joint term for the commercial timbers, without distinguishing which.
definitions.example: You should have used hardwood for this window sill instead of this junk.

definitions.definition: The sport of basketball, in particular, an indoor basketball court; so named because the floor of an indoor basketball court is normally made of hardwood.
Of a floor: made of interlocking hardwood boards.
In a frantic way.
The fact of being easy, or easily done; absence of difficulty, simplicity.

definitions.definition: The physical means or contrivances to make something (especially a public service) possible; the required equipment, infrastructure, location etc.

A musical genre related to the punk movement that makes use of electronic sounds such as synthesizers.

Teasing; tempting, especially that which is beyond reach.

Not situated on a street; e.g. off-street parking, in contrast to parking on a street.
The act of resisting, or the capacity to resist.

Electrical resistance.
An underground organisation engaged in a struggle for liberation from forceful occupation; a resistance movement.

The genus as a whole.

Clothes and vestments of such a colour.

Having a bluish-purple colour.
(often followed by up) To stop sleeping.

definitions.definition: To put in motion or action; to arouse; to excite.
To be excited or roused up; to be stirred up from a dormant, torpid, or inactive state; to be active.
To lay out a body prior to burial in order to allow family and friends to pay their last respects.
To watch, or sit up with, at night, as a dead body.
To be or remain awake; not to sleep.
To be alert; to keep watch

The act of becoming awake from sleep, or a period of time spent awake.

An elevated platform on which cannon could be placed.
An array of similar things.

A marching percussion ensemble; a drumline.
The state of a firearm when it is possible to be fired.
Apparatus for preparing or serving meals.
Clear, transparent or bright.

definitions.definition: Having a downward inclination or slope.
Predisposed, liable, inclined.

definitions.definition: With a dark appearance.
Faintly seen in the dark.

definitions.definition: In a morbid manner; morbidly, sinisterly.

definitions.definition: Mysteriously; forebodingly, ominously.

To say or express through gestures.

Grapeshot.

A person's head.

To develop a texture with small grape-like clusters of a contaminant or foreign substance.

With regard to internal affairs.
Inwardly; spiritually.

An instance of a seizure and redirection of a process.
An amendment which deletes the contents of a bill and inserts entirely new provisions.
Preflop, the position two before the dealer.
To forcibly stop and seize control of some vehicle in order to rob it or to reach a destination (especially an airplane, truck or a boat).

To seize control of a networked computer by means of infecting it with a worm or other malware, thereby turning it into a zombie.
To change software settings without a user's knowledge so as to force that user to visit a certain web site (to hijack a browser).

definitions.definition: A black rock formed from prehistoric plant remains, composed largely of carbon and burned as a fuel.

A glowing or charred piece of coal, wood, or other solid fuel.

Not kind; contrary to nature or type; unnatural.
